title: Assessing the Economy as a New Year Begins
summary: Collins opens 2025 judging the economy in a good place overall: inflation down significantly though still above target, with a healthy labor market after an orderly cooling. She supported the November and December cuts (the December one a closer call) as insurance to preserve labor-market health while keeping a restrictive stance still needed to restore price stability. She expects 2025 inflation somewhat higher than previously thought, with risks shifted to the upside, and advocates a gradual, patient, data-dependent approach with no preset path - holding longer if inflation progress stalls or easing sooner if needed. A broadly balanced, mildly dovish-leaning message.

key_quotes.quote: On balance, the December cut provided some additional insurance to preserve healthy labor market conditions while maintaining a restrictive policy stance that is still needed to sustainably restore price stability.
key_quotes.context: Rationale for supporting the December cut

key_quotes.quote: Collins expects inflation in 2025 to run somewhat higher than she previously thought, with the risks likely having shifted to the upside.
key_quotes.context: Upward revision to inflation outlook and risks

key_quotes.quote: the appropriate policy strategy is gradualism, anchored by holistic data assessment, careful analysis, and patience
my concerns about emerging labor market fragility have decreased more recently, as the unemployment rate stabilized
